summary: TypeScript-first frontend engineer with _3+ years_ shipping React apps with accessible UX, automated testing, and scalable component architecture. Comfortable in fast-moving startups and compliance-heavy environments.

projects.title: CRM Document Manager
projects.description: A law firm needed HIPAA-compliant file management inside HubSpot. I shipped a security-first solution in _5 weeks_: a React file-tree UI with move and delete flows and an iframe-based multi-file uploader with per-file progress and retries, secured by JWT auth. Now business-critical and used daily by _30+ lawyers_.

projects.title: Ohm on the Range Website
projects.description: This music festival needed a mobile-first website that drives ticket sales. I helped focus their brand and shipped a new Next.js site in _2 weeks_. It is performant, accessible, and SEO optimized, with perfect Lighthouse scores on all pages. This contributed to a _2x increase_ in traffic and ticket sales year-over-year.

experience.name: Code the Dream
experience.title: Cohort Instructional Lead
highlights: Taught _200+ students_ web development (_JavaScript, HTML/CSS, React_), translated complex topics into clear patterns, and delivered PR-style feedback on code quality and maintainability.
Produced _40+ hours_ of lectures plus learning assets (videos, infographics, articles), and partnered with staff to calibrate expectations, surface blockers early, and keep the cohort on track.

experience.name: Code for PDX
experience.title: Frontend Engineer
highlights: Contributed dozens of accessible, responsive React components to a civic-tech open-source codebase, working within an agile, TDD-enforced CI pipeline.

sideProjects.title: DogTown
sideProjects.description: Built and currently self-host a full-stack photo-sharing app on a Raspberry Pi. It includes a Next.js core with a PostgreSQL database, AI image moderation, analytics, and a system profiler that powers a live metrics dashboard for observability. It deploys through Docker Compose with CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
